title: Shabu Shabu Thinly Sliced Beef Hot Pot

seo.title: How to Make Shabu Shabu Thinly Sliced Beef Hot Pot — Easy Japanese Recipe

seo.imageAlt: Authentic Japanese Shabu Shabu Thinly Sliced Beef Hot Pot recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Shabu Shabu Thinly Sliced Beef Hot Pot — a beloved Japanese classic

description: Shabu Shabu is a beloved Japanese hot pot dish featuring thinly sliced meat and fresh vegetables cooked in a simmering broth. It's a delightful and interactive dining experience perfect for sharing with family and friends. The name "shabu shabu" is an onomatopoeia derived from the sound made when stirring the ingredients in the pot.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dashi Broth
instructions.text: In a large pot or donabe (Japanese clay pot), combine 8 cups of water and the kombu. Let it soak for 30 minutes. Heat over medium-low heat until just before boiling (around 180°F / 82°C). Remove the kombu. Add sake, soy sauce, and mirin to the dashi. Bring to a gentle simmer.

instructions.title: Arrange Ingredients
instructions.text: While the broth is heating, arrange the thinly sliced beef neatly on a large platter. Wash and prepare all vegetables: cut napa cabbage, trim mushrooms, cube tofu, and slice scallions. Arrange all ingredients attractively on separate platters around the hot pot.

instructions.title: Set Up the Dining Area
instructions.text: Place the hot pot on a portable burner in the center of your dining table. Keep the broth at a gentle simmer. Have small individual bowls for each diner, along with bowls of ponzu sauce and goma dare for dipping. Provide serving chopsticks or tongs.

instructions.title: Begin Cooking Shabu Shabu
instructions.text: Each diner uses their chopsticks to pick up a slice of beef and swish it gently in the simmering broth for just a few seconds until it changes color and is cooked to their liking. This is the "shabu shabu" motion. Enjoy the tender beef with your chosen dipping sauce.

instructions.title: Cook Vegetables and Tofu
instructions.text: After enjoying some beef, add the harder vegetables like napa cabbage and tofu to the broth first, allowing them to cook for a few minutes until tender. Then add softer vegetables and mushrooms, cooking them briefly. Continue to cook and enjoy the ingredients as you eat, replenishing as needed.

instructions.title: Enjoy the Broth and Finish
instructions.text: As you cook, the broth will become richer with the flavors of the meat and vegetables. After all ingredients are enjoyed, you can add cooked rice or udon noodles to the flavorful broth to make a delicious final soup, known as "shime". Simmer until heated through and serve immediately.

tags: Shabu Shabu Thinly Sliced Beef Hot Pot
tips: For the most authentic flavor, use high-quality, thinly sliced marbled beef. Freezing the beef briefly before slicing can make it easier to get very thin cuts if you're slicing yourself.
Don't overcrowd the pot when cooking. Swish only a few slices of meat at a time to maintain the broth's temperature and ensure even cooking.
The final broth, known as "shime," is incredibly flavorful. Add cooked rice to make zosui (rice porridge) or udon noodles for a satisfying end to your meal.
article: Shabu Shabu Thinly Sliced Beef Hot Pot is a beloved Japanese Dinner Recipes dish enjoyed across Japanese. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.

recipeIngredient: 1.5 lb (680g) Thinly sliced beef (e.g., ribeye, sirloin)
1 block (14 oz / 400g) Firm or silken tofu
as desired Cooked rice or Udon noodles
